headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: TIDE OF AI-GENERATED ACTIVITY BOOKS FLOODS THE KINDLE DRIFTS WITH MAZES THAT HAVE NO EXIT
advisory: A persistent low-content system has settled over the self-publishing storefronts, depositing children's coloring books, puzzle compendiums, and "100 Brain-Boosting Mazes" titles at a rate exceeding human authorship. Field teams report that an estimated majority of sampled mazes possess no continuous path from START to FINISH, and that several word-search grids contain none of the listed words. Residents are advised to test all puzzles before purchase and to keep a pencil and a healthy skepticism within reach. The Service notes, in the interest of full disclosure, that we are aware of the awkwardness of a machine warning you about machines.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy accumulation of 32-page activity books, many priced identically and uploaded within the same nine-minute window. Cover art shows children laughing at puzzles that cannot be completed.
Scattered "Ultimate Sudoku" releases reporting boards with two correct solutions or none. Visibility on legitimate developers reduced.
Printable maze bundles drifting in on warm engagement currents. Watermarks occasionally include a stray sixth wall in the labyrinth.
"Free printable mazes for kids" returns a dense bank of pages, each downloading a slightly melted version of the last. Expect afternoon frustration.
Calm but misleading. Pinned puzzle thumbnails look solvable from shore; conditions deteriorate sharply upon clicking through.

specimen.habitat: Print-on-demand activity books, free-printable aggregator sites, and the back pages of restaurant kids' menus where nobody checks.
specimen.field_marks: A confident START arrow, a celebratory FINISH flag, and absolutely no continuous corridor connecting the two. Walls frequently cross one another at impossible angles. The cheerful clip-art mouse standing beside it has no idea.
specimen.call: Can you help Pip the Mouse find the cheese? (Pip cannot. There is no path. There has never been a path.)
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a genuinely challenging maze drawn by a tired but well-meaning human. Tell them apart with a pencil: a real maze frustrates you, then yields. The Unsolvable Maze frustrates you, then frustrates you, then asks for a five-star review.

spotter_tip: Before buying any puzzle book, trace one maze to the end with your finger. If your finger arrives at a wall, a dead loop, or a quiet existential crisis, you have confirmed a specimen.
tomorrow: Continued heavy puzzle accumulation through midday, tapering to scattered fake crosswords by evening; bring a pencil and lower your expectations.
